question: The word 'ship' starts with which two-letter combination: 'sh', 'ch', or 'th'?
hints: Look at the first two letters of 'ship'.
solution: 'Ship' starts with the digraph 'sh'. The letters S and H work together to make one sound: /sh/.

question: Which word starts with the 'ch' sound: 'chair', 'shell', or 'three'?
hints: Look at the first two letters of each word. Which one begins with C-H?
solution: 'Chair' starts with the digraph 'ch'. 'Shell' starts with 'sh' and 'three' starts with 'th'.

question: What digraph do you see at the END of the word 'much'? Type the two letters.
hints: Look at the last two letters of 'much'.
This is the same digraph that starts 'chair' and 'chin'.
solution: The word 'much' ends with the digraph 'ch'. Digraphs can appear at the beginning or end of words.

question: Sort these words by their digraph. Which words have the 'th' digraph: 'think', 'shop', 'that', 'chip'? Type the 'th' words separated by a comma.
hints: Look at the first two letters of each word. Which ones start with T-H?
solution: 'Think' and 'that' both start with the digraph 'th'. 'Shop' has the 'sh' digraph and 'chip' has the 'ch' digraph.

question: The word 'duck' ends with the digraph 'ck'. Which other word also ends with 'ck': 'dark', 'back', or 'desk'?
hints: Look at the last two letters of each word.
'Dark' ends in R-K, 'desk' ends in S-K. What about 'back'?
solution: 'Back' ends with the 'ck' digraph, just like 'duck'. The letters C and K work together at the end of short vowel words to make the /k/ sound. 'Dark' ends in 'rk' and 'desk' ends in 'sk' — those are blends, not digraphs.
